item.title: The Softest, Fluffiest Strawberry Layer Cake
item.description: This Strawberry Layer Cake is soft, fluffy, and packed with real strawberry flavor thanks to roasting the berries and a generous dose of their freeze-dried form. It’s a beautiful make-ahead cake that feels special without being overly complicated. Serve it for birthdays, showers, or cozy spring weekends.

media:content.media:title: The Softest, Fluffiest Strawberry Layer Cake
media:content.media:description: This Strawberry Layer Cake is soft, fluffy, and packed with real strawberry flavor thanks to roasting the berries and a generous dose of their freeze-dried form. It’s a beautiful make-ahead cake that feels special without being overly complicated. Serve it for birthdays, showers, or cozy spring weekends.

item.title: Easy Homemade Arepas with Mozzarella
item.description: Arepas are made from ground corn flour and mixed with warm water. They’re most popular in Colombia and Venezuela. I like them filled with cheese and served for breakfast with huevos pericos and avocado.

media:content.media:title: Easy Homemade Arepas with Mozzarella
media:content.media:description: Arepas are made from ground corn flour and mixed with warm water. They’re most popular in Colombia and Venezuela. I like them filled with cheese and served for breakfast with huevos pericos and avocado.

item.description: Strawberry recipe collection with sweet baking ideas and snack inspiration.

item.title: The Best Easy Peasy Garlic Bread
item.description: here are the full ingredients: 1 ounce grated parm 1/2 cup (4 ounces) softened unsalted butter, pinch kosher salt, 2 tbls minced italian parsley, black pepper, pinch crushed red pepper

media:content.media:title: The Best Easy Peasy Garlic Bread
media:content.media:description: here are the full ingredients: 1 ounce grated parm 1/2 cup (4 ounces) softened unsalted butter, pinch kosher salt, 2 tbls minced italian parsley, black pepper, pinch crushed red pepper

item.title: Arroz con Pollo Recipe
item.description: A quick and simple arroz con pollo recipe.

media:content.media:title: Arroz con Pollo Recipe
media:content.media:description: A quick and simple arroz con pollo recipe.

item.title: How to make homemade limoncello
item.description:
750ml vodka 8 lemons 4 cups of water 3 cups of sugar
1. Peel the lemons and add their skins/zest to a glass container. Pour in the vodka and close to seal. Place it in a cool and dark place for at least two weeks, ideally a month. Every few days give it a shake. 2. When the limoncello concentrate is ready, make the syrup. To a medium pot, set over medium heat, add in the sugar and water. Cook until the sugar has dissolved. Transfer to the fridge to chill, at least 10-15 minutes. 3. Strain the limoncello, being sure to press down the lemon skins to release any of the limoncello. Discard the skins. Mix together the cooled simple syrup and the limoncello concentrate. 4. Transfer them to glass bottles and place labels. I divided them amongst six (6-ounce) bottles. 5. To make the spritz, add ice to a glass. Pour some limoncello in the glass along with some sparkling water and enjoy.

media:content.media:title: How to make homemade limoncello
media:content.media:description:
750ml vodka 8 lemons 4 cups of water 3 cups of sugar
1. Peel the lemons and add their skins/zest to a glass container. Pour in the vodka and close to seal. Place it in a cool and dark place for at least two weeks, ideally a month. Every few days give it a shake. 2. When the limoncello concentrate is ready, make the syrup. To a medium pot, set over medium heat, add in the sugar and water. Cook until the sugar has dissolved. Transfer to the fridge to chill, at least 10-15 minutes. 3. Strain the limoncello, being sure to press down the lemon skins to release any of the limoncello. Discard the skins. Mix together the cooled simple syrup and the limoncello concentrate. 4. Transfer them to glass bottles and place labels. I divided them amongst six (6-ounce) bottles. 5. To make the spritz, add ice to a glass. Pour some limoncello in the glass along with some sparkling water and enjoy.

item.title: Salted Honey Parker House Rolls
item.description: These Salted Honey Parker House Rolls are iconic, light and fluffy. They’re baked up until perfectly puffy and brushed with honey butter and topped with flakey sea salt. Serve them with Creamy Baked Macaroni and Cheese, Cranberry Sauce and Butterscotch Pumpkin Pie.

media:content.media:title: Salted Honey Parker House Rolls
media:content.media:description: These Salted Honey Parker House Rolls are iconic, light and fluffy. They’re baked up until perfectly puffy and brushed with honey butter and topped with flakey sea salt. Serve them with Creamy Baked Macaroni and Cheese, Cranberry Sauce and Butterscotch Pumpkin Pie.

item.title: Peruvian-Style Alfajores Recipe
item.description: This Alfajores recipe are a classic South American cookie. Silky smooth manjar blanco (dulce de leche) is sandwiched in between two melt-in-your mouth short bread cookies. Roll the edges in powdered sugar in the style of Peru. Serve them with a cup of homemade hot chocolate or matcha latte.

media:content.media:title: Peruvian-Style Alfajores Recipe
media:content.media:description: This Alfajores recipe are a classic South American cookie. Silky smooth manjar blanco (dulce de leche) is sandwiched in between two melt-in-your mouth short bread cookies. Roll the edges in powdered sugar in the style of Peru. Serve them with a cup of homemade hot chocolate or matcha latte.

item.title: Silky smooth and luscious tiramisu
item.description: This authentic Italian Tiramisu recipe consists of a sweet creamy mascarpone filling, layered with espresso-dipped lady fingers and topped with a thick dusting of cocoa powder.

media:content.media:title: Silky smooth and luscious tiramisu
media:content.media:description: This authentic Italian Tiramisu recipe consists of a sweet creamy mascarpone filling, layered with espresso-dipped lady fingers and topped with a thick dusting of cocoa powder.

item.title: A Bright and Creamy Pea and Scallop Risotto
item.description:
here's the full recipe: 4 cups chicken stock 1 tablespoon olive oil 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided 1 shallot, finely minced 3 garlic cloves, finely minced Kosher salt 1 cup Arborio rice or risotto 1/4 cup dry white wine 1 cup frozen peas, thawed Handful of pea tendrils OR spinach 3 to 4 mint leaves 1 cup finely grated Pecorino romano, divided 1/2 cup chicken stock Black pepper 2 tablespoons mascarpone cheese 1/2 lemon
Start by warming 4 cups of chicken broth in a pot. Warm some olive oil and butter in a medium pan. Add in the shallot, garlic, a few pinches of salt and cook until softened. Pour in the rice and toast for 1 minute. Deglaze the pan with some white wine. Meanwhile, in a small blender, add the peas, pea tendrils or spinach, mint leaves, 1/2 cup pecorino, chicken broth and about 1 teaspoon of salt and a few rounds of black pepper. Blend until very smooth. Set aside.
Add a few ladles of chicken broth to the risotto and let it cook until evaporated. Repeat with a few more ladles until evaporated. Repeat this process until the risotto is about 90 percent cooked. Pour in the green pea sauce, along with the remaining pecorino, 2 tablespoons butter, mascarpone cheese and lots of black pepper. Stir until combined. Give it a taste and add in a squeeze of lemon juice for some brightness. Serve with scallops, chicken or on its own.

media:content.media:title: A Bright and Creamy Pea and Scallop Risotto
media:content.media:description:
here's the full recipe: 4 cups chicken stock 1 tablespoon olive oil 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided 1 shallot, finely minced 3 garlic cloves, finely minced Kosher salt 1 cup Arborio rice or risotto 1/4 cup dry white wine 1 cup frozen peas, thawed Handful of pea tendrils OR spinach 3 to 4 mint leaves 1 cup finely grated Pecorino romano, divided 1/2 cup chicken stock Black pepper 2 tablespoons mascarpone cheese 1/2 lemon
Start by warming 4 cups of chicken broth in a pot. Warm some olive oil and butter in a medium pan. Add in the shallot, garlic, a few pinches of salt and cook until softened. Pour in the rice and toast for 1 minute. Deglaze the pan with some white wine. Meanwhile, in a small blender, add the peas, pea tendrils or spinach, mint leaves, 1/2 cup pecorino, chicken broth and about 1 teaspoon of salt and a few rounds of black pepper. Blend until very smooth. Set aside.
Add a few ladles of chicken broth to the risotto and let it cook until evaporated. Repeat with a few more ladles until evaporated. Repeat this process until the risotto is about 90 percent cooked. Pour in the green pea sauce, along with the remaining pecorino, 2 tablespoons butter, mascarpone cheese and lots of black pepper. Stir until combined. Give it a taste and add in a squeeze of lemon juice for some brightness. Serve with scallops, chicken or on its own.

item.title: Creamy, bright and delicious: Pasta al limone
item.description: Pasta al Limone is a simple summer pasta that’s creamy, bright and deliciously lemony. The combination of butter, lemon zest and juice, heavy cream and pasta water, gives you an intensely craveable pasta recipe. Serve this with my Limoncello Tiramisu and an Aperol Spritz for the most delicious Italian dinner.

media:content.media:title: Creamy, bright and delicious: Pasta al limone
media:content.media:description: Pasta al Limone is a simple summer pasta that’s creamy, bright and deliciously lemony. The combination of butter, lemon zest and juice, heavy cream and pasta water, gives you an intensely craveable pasta recipe. Serve this with my Limoncello Tiramisu and an Aperol Spritz for the most delicious Italian dinner.
